food_subgroup: Chiffon cake — oil-based sponge cake made with pandan (screwpine) leaf extract, coconut milk, egg whites, and vegetable oil; baked in a tube pan

ontology.foodon_label: chiffon cake (closest parent: sponge cake food product)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for pandan chiffon cake. FOODON_03315091 (sponge cake food product) is the closest defensible parent. No specific pandan or Singaporean chiffon cake term exists in FoodOn as of 2024.

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR (Cake) is the closest defensible parent. No specific chiffon or pandan cake code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for pandan chiffon cake. Not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of Bengawan Solo Pandan Chiffon Cake weighs approximately 80g and provides around 182 kcal, 28g carbohydrate, 6.4g fat, and 4g protein.

nutrient_source.title: Bengawan Solo Pandan Chiffon Cake — Singapore product nutrition information panel; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Cake, sponge (NDB 18101) and Singapore HPB Food Composition Database: Pandan chiffon cake
nutrient_source.organization: Bengawan Solo Singapore (primary product label); USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central (cross-reference); Health Promotion Board Singapore (cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values from Bengawan Solo Singapore product nutrition information panel (accessed 2026-04-13). Cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sponge cake (NDB 18101) and Singapore HPB Food Composition Database pandan chiffon cake entry for plausibility. Micronutrient values (vitamins, minerals) are consistent with USDA sponge cake and HPB pandan chiffon cake data. All values are within expected ranges for this food type.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sponge and chiffon-type cakes — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sponge cake and similar oil-aerated baked goods; GI ~52 for pandan chiffon cake is consistent with published GI range for sponge cakes (GI 46–54, Atkinson et al., 2008)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bengawan Solo Pandan Chiffon Cake specifically. GI ~52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sponge cake (GI 46, glucose reference) and published ranges for oil-based aerated cakes. The chiffon technique (vegetable oil, egg-white aeration) produces a lower GI than butter-based cakes due to the oil-fat matrix slowing starch digestion. GL per 80g serving = (52 × 28g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 14.6. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (150 mg/100g; 120 mg per 80g slice)
hypertension.guidance: A single 80g slice contributes approximately 120 mg sodium (5% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), making it a low sodium concern in moderation; individuals on strict sodium restriction (<1,500 mg/day) should account for cumulative intake from other sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~52 (low-medium), GL ~14.6 per 80g slice; 28g carbohydrate and 16.6g sugar per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: One 80g slice provides a manageable glycemic load of ~14.6 due to the lower GI of the chiffon technique (oil-based, egg-aerated); however, the 16.6g sugar per slice requires carbohydrate counting, and consuming two or more slices (56g carbohydrate, 33g sugar) represents a significant glycemic challenge for T2DM patients.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (2g/100g), moderate cholesterol (70 mg/100g), vegetable oil base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pandan chiffon cake has a favourable fat profile compared to butter-based cakes — saturated fat is 2g/100g versus 7–10g/100g for pound or butter cakes — and the vegetable oil base (predominantly PUFA/MUFA) is less atherogenic; cholesterol at 70 mg/100g (56 mg per slice) is moderate and within acceptable limits for most CVD patients consuming one slice occasionally.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low saturated fat; pandan leaf contains in-vitro antioxidant compounds
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vegetable oil base and low saturated fat content are consistent with an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern; pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) contains chlorophyll, alkaloids, and flavonoids with in-vitro antioxidant activity, though clinical significance at the concentration present in one cake slice is minimal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan Chiffon Cake is widely regarded as Singapore's 'national cake' — a fragrant, light, and airy sponge cake distinguished by its vivid green colour and distinctive grassy-vanilla aroma derived from fresh pandan leaf (Pandanus amaryllifolius, also called screwpine or daun pandan) juice. The cake is made using the chiffon method: egg yolks are combined with vegetable oil, coconut milk, sugar, flour, and pandan extract to form a batter, while egg whites are beaten to stiff peaks and folded in to create the characteristic light, moist crumb. The cake is baked in a tube pan and inverted to cool, producing a tall, springy texture. Bengawan Solo, Singapore's most prestigious traditional kueh and pastry bakery (established 1979 by Anastasia Liew), is synonymous with this product and sells it at Changi Airport as a Singapore souvenir. Nutritionally, per 100g: 227 kcal, 5g protein, 8g fat (2g saturated), 35g carbohydrate (20.7g sugar), 0.6g dietary fibre, 150mg sodium. The oil-based fat matrix and egg-white aeration produce a lower glycemic index (~52) than butter or pound cakes, making it a relatively better cake choice for glycemic management. A single 80g slice provides approximately 182 kcal, 28g carbohydrate, and a glycemic load of ~14.6. The cake is vegetarian and halal-certified. It is a universal Singapore birthday, celebration, afternoon tea, and daily kueh experience.
common_user_queries: is pandan chiffon cake good for diabetics

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced from Bengawan Solo Singapore product nutrition information panel, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sponge cake (NDB 18101) and Singapore HPB Food Composition Database pandan chiffon cake entry. All core macros and secondary nutrients are within expected ranges for this food type. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous sponge cake entries with documented basis. Multilingual naming complete for Singaporean cuisine requirements.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on Bengawan Solo product label and not available in Singapore HPB database for this specific product as of 2026-04-16; USDA sponge cake SR Legacy does not provide a full fatty acid profile for this oil type. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on product label or in HPB database for this specific product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label (Bengawan Solo NIP states 0g trans fat). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; pandan extract contributes negligible ascorbic acid at the concentration used. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake entries (GI 46–54); no direct GI measurement exists for Bengawan Solo Pandan Chiffon Cake specifically. GL per 80g serving calculated as (52 × 28g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 14.6. The input record listed gi_level as 'low' for GI 52; GI 52 falls at the boundary of low (≤55) and is correctly classified as low per University of Sydney GI classification. Bengawan Solo is a Singapore-registered commercial bakery; product label values are treated as primary source data consistent with Singapore Food Regulations requirements for nutrition information panels.
